name: Oral sodium butyrate increased serum butyrate and improved short-term metabolic measures
summary: The 2026 sodium butyrate trial gives direct human butyrate supplementation evidence, but in metabolic disease risk rather than aging.
statement: In 46 overweight or obese adults aged 30 to 70 years, 12 weeks of 1875 mg/day sodium butyrate increased serum butyrate; participants without diabetes lost more weight than placebo, while participants with type 2 diabetes had lower triglycerides and improved continuous-glucose-monitoring time in tight range.
population_or_model: Overweight or obese adults with and without type 2 diabetes
quantitative_note: The abstract reports weight change of -7.0 +/- 3.0 kg versus -3.2 +/- 1.6 kg in participants without diabetes, triglyceride change of -0.36 +/- 0.47 versus +0.08 +/- 0.30 mmol/L in type 2 diabetes, and a 9% increase in glucose time in tight range.
caveats: This is a small proof-of-concept metabolic trial, not an older-adult, frailty, healthspan, or disease-progression study.
Both arms received a moderately hypoenergetic diet, and the response differed by diabetes status.
The result does not establish durable aging benefit or chronic preventive safety.
